title: Playbook - Azure Logic Apps To BitSight Ratings APIs
applies_to: Logic Apps workflows that retrieve BitSight current ratings and rating history for reporting
BitSight rating ingestion pipelines that feed SharePoint or Power BI
prerequisites: Valid BitSight API token stored as a secret
Decision on whether the workflow needs portfolio snapshots, single-company history, or both
Decision on whether the destination is SharePoint, Power BI, or another persisted store
required_permissions: BitSight token access to the ratings data in scope
SharePoint or Power BI permissions appropriate to the chosen downstream path
request_sequence: Start with a Recurrence trigger for scheduled snapshots or a Request trigger for programmatic execution.
Call GET /ratings/v1/current-ratings through the Logic Apps HTTP action to retrieve the current portfolio snapshot.
If the report needs over-time data for one company, call GET /ratings/v1/companies/{company_guid} and flatten the ratings array into daily rows.
Handle pagination on current-ratings until the response indicates there are no more pages.
Normalize the response into stable columns such as guid, name, rating, rating_category, rating_as_of, and for historical rows rating_date and rating range.

Persist the normalized rows to SharePoint lists or files when the workflow needs durable history, or hand the normalized payload to a direct Power BI ingestion path if the project deliberately avoids staging.
implementation_notes: Use current-ratings for portfolio-wide current-state extracts and Company Details for one-year daily history per company.
For trend dashboards, schedule recurring extracts and store them; current-ratings alone is only a 24-hour snapshot.
SharePoint lists are the easiest durable store for normalized rating rows and scheduled snapshots. Files in a document library are useful for raw JSON or CSV archives.
Use BitSight GUID as the stable business key. Treat name as a display label only.
Because BitSight uses Basic Auth with the token as the username, the generic Logic Apps HTTP action is usually sufficient and no dedicated connector is required.
caveats: Portfolio-wide historical trends require repeated extractions over time or iterating company-by-company where the API exposes history.
The native Power BI BitSight connector is convenient for quick exploration, but its one-company limitation makes Logic Apps plus persisted storage more flexible for portfolio reporting.
Rating history beyond the one-year company-details window must come from stored extracts or separately saved report artifacts.
